page: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. The server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c6id.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU extended instructions. Single-core performance is moderate, scoring in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. The hardware excels in specialized workloads, achieving top-tier results in ray tracing, image processing, and CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is strong, ranking in the top 25% for both cached and uncached read/write operations.

The bare-metal configuration offers direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead, maximizing resource density for compute-heavy workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents an efficient allocation for compute-bound tasks, though it presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications requiring larger RAM-to-core ratios. The inclusion of 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local data access without the added latency of network-attached storage, making it a cost-effective choice for high-throughput local processing.

This server is designed for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from direct hardware access and high-speed local storage. Suitable applications include high-performance database operations, multi-threaded data processing, ray tracing, image processing, and static web serving. It is also well-suited for software compilation and moderate-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operations. While single-core performance remains average, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, ray tracing, and image processing. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and direct bare-metal access make it highly efficient for compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for high-performance databases, software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core performance.

7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
